description: This diagram illustrates the geometric relationship between two Copernicus Sentinel-1 satellite acquisitions, relevant for Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) processing. It depicts two satellite positions along an orbital path, one at time 'T' and the other at 'T+12-M Days'. The main orbital track is represented by a solid blue line within a translucent grey tube, which indicates the permissible corridor for the satellite's trajectory. A dashed orange line shows an alternative orbital path. Key geometric parameters are shown: `Bn` represents the perpendicular baseline between the two satellite positions, and `dh` denotes the perpendicular distance from the satellite at 'T+12-M Days' to the nominal orbital track (solid blue line). Grey rectangular blocks are positioned along the orbital paths, likely representing radar acquisition swaths.
